executiveSummary.text: Regime is mixed/rotational, not broad risk-off: SPY -0.13%, QQQ -1.73%, DIA +1.05%, equal-weight RSP +0.70%, VIX down to 16.15. Breadth/old-economy improved while AI-beta and Nasdaq corrected.

Rates remain the constraint: latest FRED prints show 2Y 4.17% (+3 bp), 10Y 4.48% (+4 bp), 30Y 4.97% (+6 bp), 10Y real yield 2.25% (+5 bp), breakevens stable at 2.23%. A softer jobs narrative reduced hike fear, but real yields are still high enough to pressure long-duration growth.

AI/semis are the main abnormal move: KLAC -11.5%, LRCX -10.2%, AMAT -7.4%, MU -5.5%, ASML -4.0%, SNPS -3.8%; SMH/SOX weakness spilled into Korea. This looks more like post-euphoric positioning/valuation reset than broken AI scarcity thesis.

Meta’s fresh report to sell excess AI compute reframes AI capex: bullish if hyperscalers monetize idle capacity, bearish if it signals overbuild. 2nd order: cloud pricing/utilization pressure could hit equipment/accelerator sentiment before end-demand evidence is clear.

Scarcity assets were firm but not uniform: GLD +2.03%, SLV +2.69%, copper futures +0.84%, COPX +1.81%, URNM +0.88%; WTI sits near $68.4 as the Hormuz/geopolitical premium continues to fade and supply discipline becomes the marginal driver.

Singapore remains a ballast sleeve: DBS +1.53%, OCBC +1.70%, UOB +1.06%, REITs modestly positive. High global real yields cap REIT rerating, but local banks still benefit from resilient nominal rates and wealth-management flows.

regime.label: Mixed rotation: Dow/breadth firm, Nasdaq/AI de-risking

usGlobal.what: US tape split between value/breadth and AI-duration selling.
usGlobal.why: SPY was nearly flat, Dow/equal-weight rose, while QQQ and semis sold off; VIX fell, so the move is rotation/profit-taking rather than forced deleveraging.
usGlobal.implication: Stay invested but avoid chasing the highest-beta AI winners after parabolic moves; use staged adds only on thesis names.

ratesMacro.what: Yield curve bear-steepened at the long end despite softer labor headlines.
ratesMacro.why: 2Y +3 bp, 10Y +4 bp, 30Y +6 bp; real yields rose while breakevens were stable, so the valuation pressure comes from real discount rates, not an inflation scare.
ratesMacro.implication: Long-duration equities, REITs and gold all need monitoring; gold strength despite real-yield rise suggests trust/FX demand, not just lower-rate expectation.

asiaSingapore.what: Singapore banks outperformed while REITs were modestly positive.
asiaSingapore.why: DBS/Samsung Securities announced a wealth-management partnership, and resilient rates support bank NIM/wealth income; REITs are still capped by global long-end yields.
asiaSingapore.implication: SG dividend sleeve remains ballast; add REITs only if 10Y yields roll over or spreads widen enough to compensate.

commodities.what: Precious metals and copper miners rose; oil stayed subdued near $68 WTI.
commodities.why: Gold/silver caught a bid even as real yields rose, while oil’s June geopolitical risk premium continued to deflate as Hormuz flow risk eased.
commodities.implication: Scarcity sleeve is working as a diversifier, but oil is now more about supply/demand balance than headline geopolitics.

geopolitics.what: Middle East shipping/oil risk premium is receding but not gone.
geopolitics.why: Fresh commodity coverage points to de-risking after Strait of Hormuz flow normalization; EIA-style scenarios still flag ramp-up/friction risks if conflict resumes.
geopolitics.implication: Do not buy oil simply because geopolitics exists; watch freight/insurance rates, physical flows and USD/liquidity.

moves.what: Semicap/equipment drawdown: KLAC -11.5%, LRCX -10.2%, AMAT -7.4%, ASML -4.0%.
moves.why: Fresh reports cite broad semiconductor profit-taking after a record quarter, Nasdaq weakness, and anxiety that AI capex may be closer to overbuild if hyperscalers sell excess compute.
moves.implication: Long-term wafer-fab equipment scarcity thesis intact unless orders/capex guidance weakens; near-term multiple compression can continue after parabolic gains.
moves.action: HOLD / staged ADD only if drawdown reaches pre-set valuation zones; invalidator = order cancellations, HBM/foundry capex cuts, or gross margin guide-downs.

moves.why: MU sold off despite record-results headlines as investors took profits and questioned sustainability; SNPS was pulled down with duration/semis.
moves.implication: HBM bottleneck thesis is still positive, but memory is cyclical and violent. EDA remains toll-road-like but not immune to tech multiple compression.
moves.action: HOLD; WATCH MU for HBM pricing/order durability and SNPS for EDA backlog/China restrictions.

moves.what: Financial toll roads rallied: SPGI +6.0%, Visa +3.1%.
moves.why: Rotation into quality financial infrastructure and lower perceived macro stress; HY spreads remain tight at ~2.74%.
moves.implication: Validates the non-semiconductor compounding sleeve; provides portfolio ballast when AI beta corrects.
moves.action: HOLD; ADD on broad-market pullbacks, not after single-day spikes.

moves.what: Singapore banks positive: DBS +1.53%, OCBC +1.70%, UOB +1.06%.
moves.why: Rates remain supportive and DBS announced a Samsung Securities wealth-management MOU, reinforcing fee-income optionality.
moves.implication: Dividend sleeve continues to offset US tech volatility; valuation risk rises if Singapore rates fall quickly.

moves.what: Precious metals holdings triggered commodity threshold: GLD +2.03%, SLV +2.69%.
moves.why: Demand persisted despite higher real yields and a softer USD, pointing to currency/trust diversification rather than a simple rate-cut trade.
moves.implication: Good barbell diversifier; do not overread one day as a crisis signal.
moves.action: HOLD; TRIM only if position sizing exceeds intended scarcity sleeve.

thesisImpact.why: If hyperscalers can sell excess compute, the market may question utilization before AI revenue catches up.
thesisImpact.implication: 2nd order: cloud price competition; 3rd order: delayed capex cycle for equipment if ROI disappoints. Current evidence is warning, not thesis break.
thesisImpact.action: WATCH capex guidance and cloud AI revenue conversion in upcoming earnings.

narratives.title: AI capex monetization versus overbuild
narratives.description: Meta’s reported move to sell excess AI compute can mean underutilized capacity or a new cloud revenue line. The market is repricing the probability of both at once.
narratives.source: Reuters/CNBC/Bloomberg reports within 24h via Brave Search
narratives.priceImpact: Negative for semis short term; potentially positive for hyperscaler monetization if utilization proves durable.

narratives.title: Real-yield ceiling on risk assets
narratives.description: Long-end and real yields rose even as labor data was described as softer. That means liquidity is not easing enough to justify indiscriminate duration chasing.
narratives.priceImpact: Caps Nasdaq/REIT multiples; supports cash optionality.

narratives.title: Oil geopolitical premium fades
narratives.description: Recent commodity coverage says Hormuz-related premium has mostly unwound; marginal oil driver shifts from war headlines to actual supply discipline and demand.
narratives.source: Brave Search: Business Standard/TradingKey/EIA headlines within 24h
narratives.priceImpact: Bearish/neutral oil unless physical disruptions return.

secondOrderEffects.text: Scenario weights (confidence: medium): 55% controlled rotation where AI multiples reset but credit/volatility stay calm; 25% deeper AI-duration correction if capex ROI doubts spread; 15% renewed risk-on if yields fall and earnings confirm AI monetization; 5% macro/geopolitical shock via oil/shipping or credit.
secondOrderEffects.implication: Portfolio response should be staged and probabilistic: hold core exposure, keep dry powder, and require confirming data before adding aggressively.

secondOrderEffects.text: AI selloff -> lower semiconductor multiples -> easier future returns if orders hold; but if hyperscaler compute resale pressures cloud pricing, equipment order visibility can be questioned next.
secondOrderEffects.implication: Separate valuation reset from thesis damage; monitor capex guides, not just price action.

secondOrderEffects.text: Higher real yields + lower USD + stronger gold is an unusual mix: it implies non-rate buyers may be diversifying reserve/currency exposure.
secondOrderEffects.implication: Gold’s signal is not simply 'crisis'; watch DXY, real yields and central-bank/ETF flows together.

secondOrderEffects.text: Singapore banks benefit from resilient rates, but the same rate environment delays REIT rerating.
secondOrderEffects.implication: Keep SG income barbell: banks as current yield/earnings ballast, REITs as optionality for eventual yield decline.

investment.thesis: Use AI/semicap volatility to upgrade quality, not increase junk beta.
investment.trigger: KLAC/LRCX/ASML/AMAT fall another 5-10% without capex/order deterioration, or forward multiples normalize to pre-spike ranges.
investment.whyNow: Abnormal daily moves have started to reset valuation risk after a record semis quarter.
investment.invalidator: Hyperscaler capex cuts, foundry order cancellations, or HBM demand rollover.

investment.thesis: Preserve the SGD cash/CPF/property ballast while rates remain restrictive.
investment.trigger: Deploy cash only into high-conviction drawdowns or when 10Y real yield stops rising.
investment.whyNow: Cash earns ~4% and protects FI optionality during AI-duration repricing.
investment.invalidator: Broad liquidity easing with confirmed earnings acceleration and falling real yields.

trading.thesis: Avoid catching falling knives in semis intraday; use staged limit orders only.
trading.trigger: 2-day stabilization in SMH/SOX plus VIX not rising above 20.
trading.whyNow: Selling is concentrated in crowded winners, not broad panic.
trading.invalidator: Semis keep falling while HY spreads widen and VIX breaks higher.

trading.thesis: REIT tactical add only if rates confirm lower.
trading.trigger: US 10Y breaks below 4.30% and Singapore REITs lag banks by >5%.
trading.whyNow: REITs are still rate-capped despite modest daily bounce.

watchlist.thesis: Meta/AI compute resale is the key narrative test.
watchlist.trigger: Evidence of cloud AI price discounting or weak utilization in hyperscaler earnings.
watchlist.whyNow: This is the cleanest 2nd-order threat to AI infrastructure ROI.
watchlist.invalidator: Hyperscalers show strong external AI revenue and stable capex ROI.

watchlist.thesis: Oil risk only matters if physical flows/insurance confirm it.
watchlist.trigger: Brent >$80 with rising shipping insurance/freight costs or renewed Hormuz disruption.
watchlist.whyNow: Risk premium has faded; headlines alone are insufficient.

riskWatch.name: AI capex digestion
riskWatch.description: Crowded AI winners are repricing utilization/ROI risk after extreme quarterly gains.
riskWatch.mitigation: Staged adds; prioritize balance-sheet leaders and toll roads; avoid leverage.

riskWatch.name: Real-yield/long-end pressure
riskWatch.description: 10Y real yield at 2.25% and 30Y near 4.97% keep pressure on duration equities and REIT valuations.
riskWatch.mitigation: Keep cash ballast; require valuation margin of safety for long-duration buys.

riskWatch.name: Geopolitical oil/shipping relapse
riskWatch.description: Hormuz premium has faded, but a renewed flow shock would transmit through oil, inflation expectations, Fed path and margins.
riskWatch.mitigation: Track physical flows/freight/insurance, not just headlines; maintain diversified commodity sleeve.

keyEvents.text: US jobs headlines described softer labor data, reducing near-term hike anxiety, while Fed Chair Kevin Warsh said prices remain too high.
Semiconductor stocks sold off sharply after a record quarter; CNBC reported SMH down heavily with KLAC among the largest decliners.
Meta reportedly building a cloud business to sell excess AI computing capacity.
DBS and Samsung Securities signed a wealth-management MOU to enhance Asia client services.
keyEvents.source: Business Times via Brave Search, last 24h

bottomLine.whatMatters: Scenario weights (medium confidence): 55% controlled rotation, 25% deeper AI-duration correction, 15% renewed risk-on on lower yields/earnings confirmation, 5% macro/geopolitical shock. This is a rotation/AI valuation reset inside a still-contained volatility regime. The key macro constraint is high real yields; the key portfolio test is whether AI capex converts into revenue rather than excess capacity.
bottomLine.whatToDo: Hold core VWRA/SG dividend/AI scarcity barbell; keep cash dry powder; add only in stages to highest-quality semicap/EDA names if thesis data holds. Maintain Singapore banks; be selective on REITs until rates turn.
bottomLine.whatToIgnore: Do not treat every geopolitical headline as an oil/gold buy signal, and do not confuse one-day semiconductor price damage with permanent thesis damage unless orders/capex/guidance confirm it.
